The standard semantic web format RDF (Resource Description Framework) is a W3C standard specification for representing information in the form of (subject, predicate, object) statements known as triples. This structured format, also described as (URI, property, value) for metadata, enable links between multiple data on the web using controlled vocabularies. This structured format, also described as (URI, property, value) for metadata, enable links between multiple data on the web using controlled vocabularies. The standard semantic web format RDF (Resource Description Framework) is a W3C standard specification for representing information in the form of (subject, predicate, object) statements known as triples. This structured format, also described as (URI, property, value) for metadata, enable links between multiple data on the web using controlled vocabularies. JSON-LD is widely recommended as the most suitable approach for encoding RDF metadata in your web page, learn more in the FAIR-CookBook section on search engine optimisation, or in the RDMkit section on machine actionability.
